summary: The diff implements the majority of all 7 requirements. Backend has AppUser/ManagedUser model, login, getUsers, createUser, updateUser, deleteUser. Frontend has LoginScreen, AuthContext, Header update, Sidebar admin-gating, SettingsPage with user management, and role-based gating on assets/parts/tickets pages. A few minor gaps exist around route-level redirect enforcement for /settings and the admin-only gating mechanism for backend functions.

missing_requirements.requirement: getUsers(), createUser(), updateUser(), deleteUser() are gated to admin callers only (backend enforcement)
missing_requirements.reason: The backend main.mo is truncated so it is unclear whether admin-only gating is enforced server-side for those endpoints. The login function returns a session token but there is no visible Principal-based server-side check for admin-only functions in the truncated diff. The frontend parses the token client-side only, which is insufficient for true backend gating.

missing_requirements.requirement: Regular users cannot access /settings directly (redirected away)
missing_requirements.reason: The SettingsPage uses a React useEffect redirect after render, but there is no route-level beforeLoad guard defined in App.tsx for the /settings route. A direct URL navigation by a regular user would briefly render the page before being redirected, which may not fully satisfy the AC.

hallucinated_features.description: Client management section added to the SettingsPage (add/rename/delete clients)
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only asked for a User Management section in the Settings page. The diff adds a full client registry management UI with add, edit, and delete client dialogs inside SettingsPage, which was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: backend/migration.mo file added with explicit migration support
hallucinated_features.reason: A migration module was added to the backend and the actor was configured with explicit migration. The build request made no mention of adding a migration module or enabling explicit migration.
